items.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Japanese telecommunications operator KDDI Corporation disclosed a data breach where threat actors gained access to one of its email systems used by five other internet service providers (ISPs) in the country. [...]</article>

items.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>An agentic coding tool tasked with cloning and setting up a seemingly benign GitHub repository could execute a malicious payload that remains invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human reviewers. [...]</article>

items.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>The FBI and CISA are warning that a phishing campaign targeting Signal users tied to Russian intelligence services has evolved to steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys, allowing attackers to access victims' historical messages. [...]</article>

items.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is giving federal agencies until Sunday to patch a vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server that is being actively exploited. [...]</article>

items.title: Polymarket customers lose $3 million in supply-chain attack
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Polymarket says it will fully reimburse customers who lost an estimated $3 million after hackers injected a malicious script into the platform's frontend following a breach at a third-party vendor. [...]</article>

items.title: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>Threat actors are creating OpenAI tenants that impersonate legitimate companies and inviting employees to join them, in what appears to be a ploy to trick targets into submitting sensitive company information in chats and projects. [...]</article>

items.title: Your First GRC Agent: A Red Teamer's Walkthrough
items.author: Sponsored by Anecdotes
items.content: <article>AI won't replace GRC analysts, but it can eliminate much of the repetitive work they do. Anecdotes walks through building an agent that continuously monitors controls, identifies evidence gaps, and opens remediation tasks. [...]</article>

items.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
items.author: Mayank Parmar
items.content: <article>Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]</article>

items.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks. [...]</article>

items.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>Microsoft has quietly extended its free Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program for consumers by an additional year, allowing enrolled devices to continue receiving security updates until October 12, 2027. [...]</article>

items.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>A newly discovered macOS malware dubbed "Gaslight" is designed to confuse AI-assisted malware analysis tools by hiding prompt injection strings and fake debugging data within the executable. [...]</article>

items.title: PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>A major sports piracy ring linked to the illegal PirloTV streaming platform has been disrupted in an action that targeted 44 domains. [...]</article>

items.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform continues to evolve with nearly 70 new hostnames identified over the past week and by adding browser-in-the-middle capabilities for improved data theft. [...]</article>

items.title: The Four Elevations of Effective Fraud Prevention
items.author: Sponsored by IPQS
items.content: <article>Fraudsters don't attack just one transaction. They target accounts, platforms, and entire ecosystems. IPQS explains the four elevations of fraud prevention and why broader visibility improves fraud detection. [...]</article>
